topic: Microbiome and depression — the gut-brain axis explained
caption: Did you know your gut bacteria can affect your mood? 🌟 The gut-brain axis is a complex network of interactions between your gut microbiome, brain, and nervous system. Research shows that an imbalance of gut bacteria, also known as dysbiosis, can contribute to depression. 🧬 Here are some key facts: ✨ 1 in 5 people experience depression, which is often linked to gut health. 🌿 A healthy gut microbiome produces neurotransmitters like serotonin and dopamine, regulating mood. 🧘‍♀️ Stress can disrupt the balance of gut bacteria, leading to mood changes. 🌸 Certain foods, like fermented vegetables and omega-3 rich foods, can support a healthy gut. 📚 Save this so you don't lose it. Share with someone who needs to see this. Follow @healthrecode for daily science-backed health. Don't forget — your future body will thank you! 🙏 #guthealth #mentalhealth #microbiome #depressionawareness #gutbrainaxis #healthyliving #wellness
